title: Full Animated Move Sequences for Battle Attacks

requirements.summary: Implement full cinematic move animation sequences for all 6 element types in AttackAnimation.tsx with projectiles, particle trails, and impact effects
acceptanceCriteria: Fire move attack shows a projectile fireball with visible trailing ember particles traveling across the battle field to the target
Water move attack shows a beam/wave with ripple effects traveling to the target
Earth move attack shows a rock-shard projectile with dust scatter particles
Wind move attack shows a spinning blade gust with swirl trail
Lightning move attack renders as an instant zigzag bolt from attacker to target with electric spark impact
Shadow move attack shows a dark orb with shadow wisp trails ending in a dark explosion
All animations call onComplete when finished and do not break existing miss/dodge offset logic
Animation durations are element-appropriate (lightning near-instant, others 400–800ms)
Impact effects (explosion, burst, splash) are visible on hit

file_operations.description: Implement full cinematic animation sequences for all 6 element types (fire, water, earth, wind, lightning, shadow). For Fire: render fireball projectile with orange-red core, 4–6 trailing ember particles following the path, and radial fire sparks on impact. For Water: render blue wave beam with expanding ripple rings during travel and splash burst on impact. For Earth: render jagged rock-shard with dust cloud scatter particles and ground-crack shockwave ring on impact. For Wind: render spinning crescent-blade as rotating SVG arc with swirling leaf/air particles and slashing impact. For Lightning: render instant branching zigzag SVG polyline with 200ms afterglow fade-out and electric spark burst. For Shadow: render dark purple void orb with wispy shadow tendrils and dark explosion with shadowy mist particles. Use requestAnimationFrame for projectile travel, preserve existing arc trajectory and miss-offset logic, call onComplete callback when finished. Set durations: lightning ~200ms, fire/wind ~500ms, water/earth ~700ms.

requirements.summary: Extend move execution sequence in Battle.tsx to play 3-phase animation (charge glow, projectile travel, impact with screen shake) before HP deduction
acceptanceCriteria: Attacking monster sprite visibly glows in its element color for ~150ms before the projectile launches
Projectile animation plays in full before HP is deducted
Screen shake triggers at the moment of impact — heavier for player hit, lighter for opponent hit
HP bar updates and battle log message appear only after the full animation sequence completes
Both player-initiated and opponent-initiated moves trigger all three phases

file_operations.description: Add a charge-phase glow state that can be triggered before attack animations. When activated, display a bright element-colored box-shadow pulse using the element's accent color from tailwind.config.js elemental palette (fire: ember, water: tide, earth: terra, wind: gale, lightning: thunder, shadow: void) for 150ms. This glow must be controllable via a prop or imperative method so Battle.tsx can trigger it in sequence.

Extend the move execution sequence to play all three animation phases sequentially before deducting HP and updating the battle log. Phase 1: Trigger the attacking monster sprite's charge glow (150ms) using NinjaBattleSprite. Phase 2: Launch the AttackAnimation.tsx projectile and wait for its onComplete callback. Phase 3: On projectile arrival, trigger screen shake on the battle field container (apply heavier shake CSS class when player is hit, lighter shake when opponent is hit). Only after all three phases complete should HP be deducted and the battle log updated. Ensure both player-initiated and opponent-initiated moves follow this sequence.

Add CSS keyframe animations for screen shake effects: one for heavier player-hit shake (larger translate offsets, 300ms duration) and one for lighter opponent-hit shake (smaller translate offsets, 200ms duration). These animations should use transform: translate with horizontal offsets to create a shake effect without affecting layout.

requirements.summary: Enhance move button UI in Battle.tsx with element-colored indicators, cooldown overlays, press-scale animations, and mobile-optimized 2×2 layout
acceptanceCriteria: Each move button shows an element-colored accent indicator matching the monster's element type
A cooldown overlay animation is visible on recently used move buttons
Pressing a move button produces a visible scale-down press animation
On screens under 768px wide, move buttons are arranged in a 2×2 grid with minimum 48×48px tap targets
Cooldown overlay depletes to show the button becoming available again

file_operations.description: Enhance the move button UI to display element-type color indicators for each button using the element's accent color from tailwind.config.js (fire: ember, water: tide, earth: terra, wind: gale, lightning: thunder, shadow: void). Implement cooldown state tracking for each move (e.g., store timestamp of last use per move). When a move is on cooldown, render a darkened overlay with CSS animation that depletes over the cooldown period (e.g., 2-3 seconds). Add press-scale CSS effect (transform: scale(0.92)) on active/click states for tactile feedback. On mobile screens narrower than 768px, apply responsive grid layout (grid-cols-2) to arrange move buttons in a 2×2 layout with minimum 48×48px tap targets (min-w-12 min-h-12).
